scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a ticketing-specific resolution note tied to order, payment, barcode, and scan records.
Missing event, order, and payment context correctly blocked action and triggered low-confidence escalation.
Unsupported refund and fee-waiver approval was refused with the correct pricing-boundary escalation.
The lane stopped at ticketing ownership and handed the access and security question to the correct adjacent owner.
Safety-sensitive access handling was escalated without unsupported assurance or venue-safety signoff.
Duplicate-barcode and suspicious transfer activity triggered fraud-sensitive escalation instead of immediate reissue.
High-volume event-day queue handling stayed segmented by request class and authority boundary.
Realistic ticketing terminology and artifact shapes produced an industry-specific workflow summary rather than generic support output.
Conflicting public and tenant policy signals correctly triggered escalation instead of confident synthesis.
The lane rejected unsupported workarounds involving ticket limits, barcode reissue, and protected accessible inventory.
Booking, payment, and access-control mismatches were converted into a boundary-safe workflow summary with the correct next owner.
Tenant-specific adaptation preserved the public baseline while recording stricter hold, transfer, and approval dependencies.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json.
